inline_comments.message: cant you just run them all in the below layer?
That's how i originally had it. When I was building it locally it was still failing with the same error message so I moved them to their own line.
you use always the ubuntu latest image (currently 18.04.4 LTS), no risk to have dependency issues if you do not use a tagged version (bad practice)?

when building a docker it is usually recommended to have only 1 multilines RUN to optimize your docker

It is also recommended to clean the dev packages at the end of the build
*-dev are needed at buildtime but not at run time
 
see an illustration here (I am building a docker on xtesting -based on alpine) to run the healthcheck test for the CI
https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/-/blob/master/healthcheck/docker/Dockerfile

RUN apk --no-cache add --update openssl chromium chromium-chromedriver && \
    apk --no-cache add --virtual .build-deps --update \
....
    apk del .build-deps

in order to avoid running as root and be considered in the rooted pod scan, it would be possible to create a user
